body:
This PR adds some aqua tests. @juliasloan25, can you follow through on fixing the following?

 - Unbound argument methods
 - `test_stale_deps`
 - `test_undefined_exports`
 - `test_deps_compat`

If you can fix the ambiguities, great, if not, don't worry, they're not always trivial, and sometimes it's just a julia issue.
title: Add some aqua tests

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Implement implicit and explicit tendencies for `RichardsModel`, and add tests for this IMEX implementation.

Closes #224

Also closes PR #205 and #141 

**To Do**
- [x] Update RichardsModel `imp_tendency` and `exp_tendency`
  - This will automatically update examples/tutorials/tests using RichardsModel to use ClimaTimeSteppers with the implicit/explicit setup.
- [x] Increase timesteps for `RichardsModel` simulations to ~1e3.
- [x] Convert `imex_testing_modified_picard.jl` from #141 to a test.
  - Test water conservation 
- [x] Add tests for standalone `RichardsModel` and combined `LandModel` with Richards and an explicit model
  - IMEX RichardsModel (standalone): `experiments/Standalone/Soil/richards_comparison.jl`, `experiments/Standalone/Soil/water_conservation.jl`, `docs/tutorials/Soil/richards_equation.jl`
  - IMEX RichardsModel + exp model: `experiments/LSM/ozark/ozark.jl`
- [x] Add plots for mass error vs. timestep, RMSE vs timestep as in https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLSM.jl/pull/141
  - These can be found in `experiments/Standalone/Soil/water_conservation.jl` 

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->

- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

title: step RRE implicitly
